title: Phytoplankton and bacteria filtration
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2ss4t","text":"For use in \"Isolation of cyanophages by liquid bioassays\"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":12,"length":44}],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"5683A58F304140C5B80CE2A76B01AD4D","url":"https://www.protocols.io/view/Isolation-of-cyanophages-by-liquid-bioassays-dp25qd"}}}}
steps: Filter sample using glass fiber filter (e.g., GC50, Advantec) This step may help to reduce premature clogging of the next filter.
Then filter with 0.2 µm or 0.45 µm PVDF filter (e.g., Millipore Durapore filters). As for the bacteriophage isolation, this filtration step may also remove some of the larger viral particles.
creator: Amy Chan
